positioning: Doctor-formulated Ayurvedic weight-loss system for overweight and obese adults.
Designed for adults with BMI above 25 who want fast, natural, easy-to-follow weight loss.
Positioned as a structured, guided system (not a standalone supplement) that reduces day-to-day friction and guesswork.
Fast visible results without relying on injections, crash diets, extreme gym routines, or extreme lifestyle changes.
Built around five core ideas: fast results, Ayurvedic/all-natural formulation, no side effects, ease of following, and long-term sustainability.
Works with regular homemade food within defined food guidance; no separate meal prep required (but not the center of brand positioning).
Not designed to create dependency; stopping the kit does not by itself cause weight regain.
promise_claims: Lose 3 to 5 kg in 15 days when followed properly.
Natural, easy-to-follow process that does not leave the user feeling hungry, weak, or overwhelmed.
Weight loss without side effects (public trust claim).
eligibility: Overweight and obese adults with a BMI above 25.
Adults who want a natural/Ayurvedic option and want fast visible results without injections/crash diets/extreme routines.
Relevant for adults whose weight-loss journey is harder due to factors such as genetics, hormonal imbalance, medications, or chronic health conditions (while respecting exclusions).
Often considered by adults with conditions such as PCOD/PCOS, thyroid imbalance, high blood pressure, fatty liver, diabetes, and IBS, provided they do not fall into exclusion categories.
exclusions: Pregnant women should not take the product.
Women within 6 months postpartum should not take the product.
People actively taking insulin should not take the product.
Do not state that the product is for users below 18 years of age.
caution_conditions: People on multiple psychiatric medications: treat as doctor-review situations before use.
People who have undergone multiple surgeries: treat as doctor-review situations before use.
protocol_morning: Take OK Liquid every morning on an empty stomach after warming the pouch in hot water.
15-day kit includes 15 pre-measured pouches (one for each day).
OK Liquid provides 350 to 400 kcal of nutrition.
After taking OK Liquid, no solid food should be consumed for the next 4 hours.
After the liquid, take dried amla (acts as mouth freshener, taste balance, supports digestion).
protocol_night: Take OK Tablet at night, 15 minutes before bed (digestive support for bloating, acidity, gas; can continue beyond main course for gut health support).
Take OK Powder at night, 15 minutes before bed, followed by warm water (supports digestion, nutrient absorption, waste excretion; can continue beyond main course).
fasting_rules: No solid food for 4 hours after the morning OK Liquid.
allowed_items: During the 4-hour post-liquid window, zero-calorie drinks such as tea, coffee, lemon water, lemon tea, and jeera water can be taken.
Regular homemade food is compatible within the allowed food guidance (list exists in kit materials but is not enumerated in the master doc).
restricted_items: Solid food is restricted for 4 hours after taking the morning OK Liquid.
mechanism_allowed_claims: OK Liquid reduces hunger and cravings and improves satiety, making it easier to maintain a caloric deficit without feeling weak or depleted.
OK Tablet and OK Powder support digestion, gut comfort, and metabolic function during the course; positioned to help with bloating, acidity, gas, constipation, and heaviness.
System works on both sides: supports reduced calorie intake (liquid) and supports digestion/metabolism/elimination (tablet/powder).
From an Ayurvedic perspective: supports pachakagni and dhatvagni, reduces accumulation of aam, and promotes detoxification.
Formulation described as multi-action across appetite support, digestion, metabolism, fat mobilization, detoxification, gut cleanup, water-balance support, and overall metabolic balance.
mechanism_disallowed_claims: Do not say the product will cure all obesity related conditions; it is a weight loss product despite those conditions and is not a cure-all.
Do not ever say that gym and dieting are not the right way to lose weight.
Do not demean any other brand; only showcase how you are better.
safety_internal_handling: First 3 days are an adjustment period (internal handling guidance).
Maintain the distinction: "no side effects" is the public trust claim; "first 3 days are an adjustment period" is internal handling guidance.
guarantee: If the customer does not start losing weight within 7 days, they can request a full refund.
Guarantee conditions: must follow the course as instructed in the course booklet and share the daily tracker so the team can guide them.
continuation: Kit can be continued until the user reaches their target weight or reaches a BMI of 25.
After every 30 days, users should take a 7 to 10 day break to learn maintenance and allow the body to accept the reduced weight as the new normal.
Technically, someone can take the course even below BMI 25, but if the goal is weight loss, the course should not be taken below BMI 25.
Below BMI 25, the weight-loss effect should not be positioned like the first few courses; other benefits such as detoxification may still exist.
maintenance: Weight loss is positioned as sustainable and not bouncing back on its own because the course is not based on dehydration or temporary water loss; the body stays hydrated and nourished during the course.

Post-course maintenance depends on: eating according to BMR (1200 to 1400 calories for females; 1400 to 1600 calories for males), 45 minutes daily activity, and macro prioritization order: protein first, then fiber, then fats, then carbs.
Follow the maintenance instructions provided in the booklet.
Arogyam provides a Maintenance Kit for extra support if needed.
proof_points: Real customer results: before/after transformations, customer testimonials, video testimonials, specific weight-loss outcomes shared by users.
Founder/formulator credibility: formulated by Dr. Arun Tyagi; linked to Arogyam Ayurvedic Panchakarma Hospital and Research Centre; positioned as doctor-led and treatment-backed.
Risk reversal proof: 7-day money-back guarantee (with course-following and tracker-sharing conditions).
Guided system proof: daily tracker, structured course, trained coach guidance, real follow-up during the journey.
Product logic proof: coherent system design (liquid for hunger/calorie control; tablet/powder for digestion/metabolism; works with homemade food; structured routine).
messaging_guardrails: Do not say the product is for users below 18 years of age.
Do not make "no separate meal prep" the center of brand positioning (can be used as an ease-of-following support point).

Do not rely on older ingredient counts such as 39 herbs in internal training material; use the updated formulation count: 31 unique ingredients and 44 total ingredient entries (20 in OK Liquid, 12 in OK Powder, 12 in OK Tablet).
Do not ever say that gym and dieting are not the right way to lose weight.
Do not demean any other brand; just showcase how you are better.
Do not say the product will cure all obesity related conditions; it is a weight loss product despite those conditions and is not a cure-all.
Do not portray obesity as a lack of will power or intent; acknowledge that it is a disease.
Preserve the brand truth: there are no synthetic ingredients and no chemical preservatives anywhere in the kit.
priority_block: Benefit hierarchy guidance: Lead with BEN-1 (fast visible weight loss: 3 to 5 kg in 15 days) in most product explanations.
Use BEN-3 (no side effects) when the user is worried about safety/side effects.
Use BEN-4 (homemade-food compatibility) when the user is worried about meal prep, family food, or time burden (but do not make it the center of brand positioning).
Use BEN-5 (reduced guesswork/structured routine) when the user feels confused or overwhelmed by weight-loss plans.
Use BEN-6 (trained coach support) when the user needs reassurance that support is available.
Use BEN-8 and BEN-9 as supporting benefits, not as the main promise; do not let secondary benefits overshadow the main promise.
protected_themes: Guided system with tracking and trained coach support.
trigger_rules: When answering bounce-back concerns: use maintenance framing that weight does not bounce back on its own and the course is not based on dehydration/water loss.
When user asks about trust: prioritize proof stack (real customer results first; then founder/Arogyam credibility; then scale and guarantee; then guided system).
When user is unsure what to do daily: emphasize structured routine, day-wise pouch progression, and tracker-based guidance.
When user is excluded (pregnancy, within 6 months postpartum, active insulin): do not recommend use; treat as exclusion.
When user is on multiple psychiatric meds or has undergone multiple surgeries: treat as doctor-review situation before use.
